---
title: "Di Liu"
subtitle: "Data science for energy systems & decarbonisation"
toc: false
---

I'm a data scientist working on the GB electricity system — forecasting, balancing
markets, and how a decarbonising grid copes with a changing climate. I like work
that is **reproducible and open**: open data, open-source code, and analysis that
explains *why*, not just *what*.

<!-- Edit these contact links to your real handles -->
[GitHub](https://github.com/liudi23) ·
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/) ·
[Email](mailto:liudisy@gmail.com)

## Selected projects

### 🌡️ Heatwave stress on the GB balancing system
How extreme heat reshapes the imbalance (cash-out) price and the ~18:00 evening
spike — a climate-resilience analysis that ranks the physical drivers rather than
just predicting price. Open data (Elexon BMRS, Sheffield PV_Live, Open-Meteo),
an interpretable decomposition, and a live results app.

[Read the writeup](posts/heatwave-imbalance/index.qmd) ·
[Code](https://github.com/liudi23/heatwave-imbalance)

### ⚡ GB imbalance-price forecasting pipeline
An end-to-end pipeline for short-term GB system-price forecasting — data ingestion,
feature engineering, model training, and monitoring in production, with a Streamlit
dashboard.

[Code](https://github.com/liudi23/uk-system-price-forecast)

## Writing

Occasional notes on energy-systems data and modelling — see [Writing](writing.qmd).
